statement: Causal, dependency, or explanatory relationships exist between elements of the phenomenon.
The investigation's procedures produce effects on the investigation itself or its object.
Some explanations are better than others — there is a basis for preferring one account.
The investigation can in principle reach a stopping point or recognize when further investigation is unproductive.
External reference is in principle possible — something exists outside the investigation's current frame.
The investigation can have knowledge about its own state and outputs.
The investigation can know when it has reached a limit of its current method or framework.
The investigation can distinguish between different causal explanations that fit the same observations.
The investigation can communicate its results and compare them across different formulations.
There is information about the phenomenon that the investigation can extract and process.
The available information about the phenomenon is finite.
Processing information can change it — information can be lost, transformed, or degraded.
The investigation is a process with identifiable stages, phases, or states.
The investigation produces determinate results — outputs that can be compared across phases.
The investigation's trajectory can be represented abstractly (state space, sequence, graph).
Multiple legitimate methods or approaches exist for investigating the phenomenon.
The choice of method constrains or shapes what can be discovered.
Methodological choices can be changed, and their effects observed.
The phenomenon under study has some stable structure — it is not pure randomness or noise.
The phenomenon and the investigation of it are distinguishable (even if inseparable in practice).
There exists an inside/outside boundary relative to the investigative system.
The target has properties that are not entirely constituted by the investigation of it.
The investigation can examine its own outputs as objects of analysis.
The investigation can reflect on its own procedures and methods.
The investigation can detect recurrence — whether it has been in its current state before.
